html:
Exclusive to:

The Dreadstone Armor is a craftable chest slot Armor, crafted in either the Armor or Magic Filter. It requires 6 Dreadstone and 4 Pure Horror to craft. The Rare Blueprint for the Dreadstone Armor is dropped after defeating the Nightmare Werepig.

It has a Durability of 840 (over twice as durable as a Log Suit). It absorbs 90% of received physical damage and 5 flat received Planar Damage.

An extra 10% protection is added after the initial 90% (total 91%) to physical protection from Shadow Aligned creatures. When combined with the Dreadstone Helm or Void Cowl, the total physical damage protection against Shadow Aligned creatures increases to 92%, as the extra protection added after the initial 90% becomes 20% instead of 10%.

Additionally, 10% Planar Protection is added before the flat Planar Defense against Shadow Aligned creatures. When combined with the Dreadstone Helm or Void Cowl, this bonus becomes 20%. This manner of protection is much more effective than the physical damage counterpart.

While worn at less than 100% durability, the Dreadstone Armor drains 20 /min. If the Dreadstone Helm is also equipped, however, it drains -10 /min.

Contents

  • 1 Durability
  • 2 Skins
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Assets
  • 5 Gallery

Durability

Armor restores durability every second by a certain amount, which depends on the amount of sanity of the character and is calculated by the formula: P=11000+(428.57*t)*b

P - is the percentage of recovery as a decimal;

t - is the percentage of the character's sanity as a decimal fraction;

b - bonus from having dreadstone helm also equipped, which is equal to 1.5 if equipped, and 1 if not.

The following are examples of restoring durability to full sanity and lack thereof.

Sanity Recovery rate With a bonus
100% 0.0007/s

1% per 14s

0.001/s

1% per 10s

0% 0,001/s

1% per 10s

0,0015/s

1% per 6,7s

Dreadstone Armor and Dreadstone Helms do not regenerate durability while Enlightenment is active. This makes the armor less preferable for the Celestial Champion fight or any other lunar related combat, though their incredible durability and sanity drain makes them useful so long as the player is mindful of the armor's durability.

Become the most dreaded warrior on the battlefield.

Trivia

  • Maxwell and Old Wanda do not lose their sanity when wearing Dreadstone Armor.

Exclusive to:
“Lightweight, sturdy, and snazzy!”
–Wilson
“It's fine I guess.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang doesn't need scary rock armor!”
–Wolfgang
“Let the darkness surround me.”
–Wendy
“IT LOOKS GOOD ON ME”
–WX-78
“Extremely durable, and surprisingly lightweight.”
–Wickerbottom
“Lucy says black ain't my colour.”
–Woodie
“Black never goes out of style.”
–Maxwell
“It will serve me well, despite its örigins.”
–Wigfrid
“It's kinda scary, but it keeps us safe!”
–Webber
“A fine peice[sic] of armor.”
–Warly
“Pretty rock clothes”
–Wormwood
“That's some real high-grade protective equipment.”
–Winona
“All blows will be blocked by this armor of rock.”
–Wortox
“So pretty... it mine!”
–Wurt
“It looks like an evil knight's armor. Neat!”
–Walter
“It should keep me well protected, at least.”
–Wanda

It requires 6 [[Dreadstone]] and 4 [[Pure Horror]] to craft. The [[Blueprint|Rare Blueprint]] for the Dreadstone Armor is dropped after defeating the [[Nightmare Werepig]]. It has a [[Durability]] of 840 (over twice as durable as a [[Log Suit]]). It absorbs 90% of received physical damage and 5 flat received [[Planar Damage]]. An extra 10% protection is added ''after'' the initial 90% (total 91%) to physical protection from [[Shadow Aligned]] creatures. When combined with the [[Dreadstone Helm]] or [[Void Cowl]], the total physical damage protection against Shadow Aligned creatures increases to 92%, as the extra protection added after the initial 90% becomes 20% instead of 10%. Additionally, 10% Planar Protection is added ''before'' the flat Planar Defense against Shadow Aligned creatures. When combined with the Dreadstone Helm or Void Cowl, this bonus becomes 20%. This manner of protection is much more effective than the physical damage counterpart. While worn at less than 100% durability, the Dreadstone Armor drains 20 {{Sanity}}/min. If the Dreadstone Helm is also equipped, however, it drains -10 {{Sanity}}/min. =={{Pic|32|Log Suit}}Durability== Armor restores durability every second by a certain amount, which depends on the amount of sanity of the character and is calculated by the formula: P = \dfrac{1}{1000 + (428.57*\text{t})} * b P - is the percentage of recovery as a decimal; t - is the percentage of the character's sanity as a decimal fraction; b - bonus from having [[Dreadstone Helm|dreadstone helm]] also equipped, which is equal to 1.5 if equipped, and 1 if not. This makes the armor less preferable for the [[Celestial Champion]] fight or any other lunar related combat, though their incredible durability and sanity drain makes them useful so long as the player is mindful of the armor's durability. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Armor_Items|Belongings}}
